description:
A security audit is being performed on the “project” workspace that
    has already been staged for you under

        /home/user/project

    The auditor needs a **plain-text log file** that captures the current
    Unix permissions for every file and directory in that tree
    (including the root of the tree itself).

    Your job:
    1. Inspect everything located at /home/user/project recursively.
    2. Generate a report named

           /home/user/permission_audit.log

       that follows **exactly** the format described below.
    3. The report must overwrite any existing file of the same name.

    Required report format (this will be used verbatim by automated
    grading scripts):

    • One entry per line.  
    • Each line must contain:  
          a. the numeric (octal) permission bits, **without** a leading
             zero (e.g., 755, 644, 700)  
          b. a single space character  
          c. the absolute path of the file or directory  
    • No extra spaces or characters.  
    • Lines must be sorted in **strict ASCII alphabetical order** by the
      absolute path.  
    • Do not include anything outside /home/user/project.  
    • Do not include blank lines.

    Example of a correctly-formatted line (do *not* hard-code this):
        644 /home/user/project/example.txt

    When you have finished, the grader will read
    /home/user/permission_audit.log and compare it against the expected
    contents. Make sure the file exists and matches the specification
    precisely—character-for-character.
truth:
Initial filesystem state (already present before the agent starts):

      1. Directory  /home/user/project                     (mode 0755)
      2. File       /home/user/project/config.cfg          (mode 0644)
      3. Directory  /home/user/project/scripts             (mode 0755)
      4. File       /home/user/project/scripts/build.sh    (mode 0750)
      5. Directory  /home/user/project/data                (mode 0700)
      6. File       /home/user/project/data/raw.txt        (mode 0600)

    No /home/user/permission_audit.log file exists initially.

    Correct final state after a successful run:

      File /home/user/permission_audit.log exists and its exact
      byte-for-byte contents are:

        755 /home/user/project
        644 /home/user/project/config.cfg
        700 /home/user/project/data
        600 /home/user/project/data/raw.txt
        755 /home/user/project/scripts
        750 /home/user/project/scripts/build.sh

      (The final trailing newline character that a typical POSIX text
      file ends with **is** required.)
